ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for braised beef noodle set meal; mapped to broader 'noodle dish' class (FOODON_00002473) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle soup dishes — wheat noodles in broth with meat
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for braised beef noodle set meal; A0CKP (noodle soup with meat) used as the closest defensible analogous category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this composite dish.

serving.description: 1 set meal (noodles, braised beef, broth, and a beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal serving of approximately 800 g includes a bowl of wheat noodles with braised beef in savoury broth plus an accompanying beverage, as commonly served at Singaporean Chinese noodle stalls and restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB entry exists for a full braised beef noodle set meal. Per-100g values are derived from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) cross-referenced against HPB Foodo component entries for beef noodle soup, braised beef, and wheat noodles. Values are treated as estimated composites. The set meal beverage component is not separately itemised; nutrient values reflect the noodle-and-beef-broth bowl component weighted across the full 800 g serving. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for braised beef (NDB 13365) and wheat noodles (NDB 20110) were used to validate macro plausibility.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a braised beef noodle set meal. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on the analogous entry for wheat noodles (boiled, white, GI ~52, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008). The beef and broth components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (52 × 80.0 g carbohydrate in 800 g serving) / 100 = 41.6. High GL reflects the large 800 g set meal portion size.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from beef broth and soy sauce; estimated 312.5 mg sodium per 100 g equates to approximately 2,500 mg per 800 g set meal serving, approaching or exceeding the 2,300 mg daily limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request low-sodium broth and reduced soy sauce, choose water or unsweetened tea as the set meal beverage, and limit portion size; actual restaurant sodium is likely higher than the estimated 312.5 mg/100 g due to broth concentration.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (52) wheat noodles with low dietary fibre (0.63 g/100 g, well below the 3 g/100 g threshold) and a high glycemic load of 41.6 per 800 g serving result in significant postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute wheat noodles with konjac or wholemeal noodles to reduce GI and increase fibre, request a smaller noodle portion with more protein and vegetables, and choose water or unsweetened tea as the set meal beverage to avoid additional sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 1.88 g/100 g (approximately 15 g per 800 g serving) from braised beef brisket or shank, combined with high sodium (~2,500 mg per serving) and trans fat (0.06 g/100 g), presents a combined cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request leaner beef cuts or substitute with tofu, choose low-sodium broth, avoid fried or sugary set meal beverages, and increase vegetable portions to improve the fibre and potassium profile of the meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef shank or brisket is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6) in rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute beef with tofu, tempeh, or seafood to reduce pro-inflammatory omega-6 load while retaining the anti-inflammatory spice broth (ginger, cinnamon, star anise) and bok choy (sulforaphane source).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised Beef Noodles Set Meal (红烧牛肉面套餐) is a popular Chinese-style set meal widely available at Singaporean noodle stalls, food courts, and casual restaurants. The dish centres on a generous bowl of springy wheat noodles submerged in a deeply savoury, aromatic beef broth that has been slow-braised with soy sauce, star anise, cinnamon, ginger, and Shaoxing wine. Tender slices or chunks of braised beef — typically shank or brisket — are placed on top, accompanied by blanched bok choy and garnished with spring onions and sometimes chilli oil. The set meal format includes an accompanying beverage, which may be hot or iced Chinese tea, barley water, or a soft drink depending on the vendor. The broth is rich, umami-forward, and slightly sweet from the braising liquid, while the noodles provide a chewy, satisfying texture. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 106 kcal per 100 g, with 6.9 g protein, 4.4 g fat, and 10 g carbohydrate. The full 800 g set meal delivers roughly 850 kcal. Sodium content is high at approximately 312.5 mg/100 g (estimated ~2,500 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Dietary fibre is low at 0.63 g/100 g, and the low GI of approximately 52 combined with a high glycemic load of 41.6 per serving makes this dish unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without modification. The dish is not vegetarian, not vegan, and contains gluten (wheat noodles) and soy (soy sauce) allergens. It is not certified halal due to the use of beef and potential cross-contamination. Anti-inflammatory spices in the broth (ginger, cinnamon, star anise) provide minor beneficial phytochemical contributions, but these are insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid load from red meat for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a beef noodle set meal?
Is beef noodle soup suitable for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of braised beef noodles?
Can I eat beef noodles if I have high blood pressure?
Is braised beef noodle set meal halal?
What allergens are in beef noodle soup?
How much protein is in braised beef noodles?
Is beef noodle soup good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Per-100g nutrient values are derived from an ai_generated input record (confidence 0.7) cross-referenced against HPB Foodo component entries and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for braised beef and wheat noodles. No single approved-source entry exists for the complete set meal. Macro plausibility is confirmed but exact values remain estimated composites. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat noodles.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite braised beef noodle set meal is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry as of 2024. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not reported in the input record and no single approved-source composite entry is available; estimated ~190 mg/100 g from bok choy and spring onion components but not confirmed by an approved source, so -1 is used. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the input record and are not available for this specific composite dish in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. The set meal beverage component (type unspecified) is included in the 800 g portion weight but its nutrient contribution is not separately itemised; if the beverage is a sugar-sweetened drink, actual sugar and sodium values per serving will be materially higher than reported. Halal status is unconfirmed; beef sourcing and preparation method at individual vendors may or may not meet halal certification requirements. Trans fat reported at 0.06 g/100 g from the input record; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with naturally occurring trans fats in ruminant beef products.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input. Added Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi local names to satisfy G6 Singaporean dish multilingual requirement. Added GI estimate (52, medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for boiled wheat noodles. Added GL calculation (41.6 per 800 g serving). Added cardiovascular_disease health context condition (missing from input). Renamed 'diabetes' condition key to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes'. Added ontology section with FoodOn and FoodEx2 analogous entries and gap notes. Added full per_100g field set with -1 sentinels for unavailable micronutrients. Documented all -1 values in notes. Added LLM natural language description exceeding 200 characters. Verified aliases consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases array.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' and 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' as per validator feedback. Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to align with glycemic_index 52 (GI 55 or less is considered low). Updated relevant text in gi_source and llm_training. Self-scored draft. All 6 hard gates pass. All 8 Q criteria pass. Score 8/8. Decision: PASS.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
